features.description: Fix next/prev buttons in NowPlayingBar navigating to the track page. In components/NowPlayingBar.tsx, there is a `navigatingRef` pattern (line 32) where clicking next/prev sets `navigatingRef.current = true`, then a useEffect (lines 44-49) watches `currentTrack` and does `router.push` when navigatingRef is true. This causes unwanted page navigation when the user just wants to skip tracks. FIX: (1) Remove `const navigatingRef = useRef(false)` on line 32. (2) Remove the useEffect on lines 44-49 that checks navigatingRef. (3) Change the prev button onClick (line 162) from `() => { navigatingRef.current = true; prev(); }` to just `() => prev()`. (4) Change the next button onClick (line 184) from `() => { navigatingRef.current = true; next(); }` to just `() => next()`. (5) Remove `useRef` from the React import if no other refs use it (check the rest of the file). KEEP the `setOnTrackChange` callback (lines 37-42) — that handles auto-advance countdown navigation which SHOULD navigate. DONE: Next/prev buttons skip tracks without navigating away from the current page. Auto-advance countdown still navigates. DO NOT TOUCH: the setOnTrackChange callback, the Link component wrapping track info, any other files. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Remove the useEffect that watches navigatingRef + currentTrack
Simplify prev onClick to just call prev()
Simplify next onClick to just call next()
Check if useRef is still needed — if not, remove from import

features.description: Fix play count flash on the /songs page only. In app/songs/page.tsx, play counts load from localStorage first (flash of stale data), then Supabase overwrites. If Supabase returns 0 or fails, the count disappears — creating a visible flash. FIX: (1) Change `useState<Record<string, number>>({})` to `useState<Record<string, number> | null>(null)`. (2) Remove the localStorage load block (the try/catch around lines 28-31 that reads from `lgh-play-counts`). Keep only the Supabase fetch. (3) When rendering play counts (around line 197), guard with `playCounts !== null` before accessing `playCounts[track.slug]`. Only show count if `playCounts && (playCounts[track.slug] || 0) > 0`. (4) The `sortBy === 'most-played'` sort also uses playCounts — guard it: treat null as empty object for sorting purposes. DONE: Play counts on /songs page appear only after Supabase responds. No flash. DO NOT TOUCH: the Supabase fetch itself, other pages, /api/play endpoint. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Change playCounts state type to include null
Guard all playCounts renders with null check
Guard the most-played sort with null check

features.description: Fix play count flash on the album page only. In app/album/[slug]/AlbumPageClient.tsx, play counts load from localStorage first (flash of stale data), then Supabase overwrites. FIX: (1) Change `useState<Record<string, number>>({})` (line 32) to `useState<Record<string, number> | null>(null)`. (2) Remove the localStorage load block (the try/catch around lines 123-126 that reads from `lgh-play-counts`). Keep only the Supabase fetch. (3) When rendering play counts (around line 413), guard with `playCounts !== null` before accessing. Only show count if `playCounts && (playCounts[track.slug] || 0) > 0`. DONE: Play counts on album pages appear only after Supabase responds. No flash. DO NOT TOUCH: other pages, the Supabase fetch logic, /api/play endpoint. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Change playCounts state type to include null
Guard play count render with null check

features.description: Fix play count flash on the track detail page only. In app/track/[slug]/TrackPageClient.tsx, play count loads from localStorage first (flash), then Supabase overwrites. FIX: (1) Change `useState(0)` (line 72) to `useState<number | null>(null)`. (2) Remove the localStorage load block (the try/catch around lines 76-79 that reads from `lgh-play-counts`). Keep only the Supabase fetch. (3) When rendering play count (around line 224), only show if `playCount !== null && playCount > 0`. DONE: Play count on track pages appears only after Supabase responds. No flash. DO NOT TOUCH: other pages, the Supabase fetch logic, /api/play endpoint. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Change playCount state type to number | null, initial null
Guard play count render with null check

features.description: Fix date column alignment on /songs page. In app/songs/page.tsx, the 'Date added' column header has `text-right` but date values in track rows are in a flex container with other elements (play count, duration) that don't have fixed widths, so the date column shifts depending on whether play count is shown. FIX: (1) In the column headers row (around line 86-93), add a play count header between Title and Date: `Plays`. Keep the date header but give it `w-20`. (2) In the track row's right-side container (around line 196-210), give each element a fixed width: play count `w-20`, date `w-20`, duration `w-12`. CRITICAL: Always render the play count span even when count is 0 — just render an empty span with the same width class to maintain column alignment. Only show the number text inside when count > 0. DONE: All columns align perfectly regardless of which tracks have play counts. DO NOT TOUCH: track row click behavior, sort logic, play count fetch logic, other pages. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Add Plays column header with w-20
In track rows, give play count span w-20 (always rendered, text conditional)

features.description: Fix duplicate annotation highlighting on repeated lyric lines. In components/SyncedLyrics.tsx, the `findAnnotation()` function (lines 93-100) matches annotations by substring. If the same lyric line appears multiple times (e.g., 'Tell me what hard means to you' repeats 7+ times in 'the-one-question'), EVERY instance gets the annotation icon, making it look spammy. FIX: (1) Create a new `useMemo` that pre-computes a `Map<number, LyricAnnotation>` (line index → annotation). Iterate through `lines` once. For each non-section line, call `findAnnotation`. If it returns a match, check if that annotation's index in the `annotations` array has already been used (track with a `Set<number>`). If not used, add to the map and mark as used. If already used, skip — that line gets no annotation. (2) Replace the inline `findAnnotation` call in the render loop (line 156) with a simple map lookup: `annotationMap.get(i)`. (3) Update `annotationCount` to use `annotationMap.size` instead of filtering lines. (4) Update the auto-expand logic (the useMemo around line 130) to find the first key in annotationMap instead of calling findAnnotation. DONE: Each annotation icon appears exactly once — on the first lyric line that matches. Subsequent identical lines have no icon. The count in the banner reflects unique matched annotations. DO NOT TOUCH: annotation expand/collapse behavior, share lyric feature, parseLyrics function. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Create useMemo for annotationMap: Map<number, LyricAnnotation>
Iterate lines, use Set to track used annotation indices, build map
Replace inline findAnnotation in render with annotationMap.get(i)

features.description: Add search to the /songs page. Currently app/songs/page.tsx has sort pills but NO search input. FIX: (1) Add `const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('')`. (2) Add a search input between the sort pills div and the column headers div. Style it like the homepage search bar in app/page.tsx (lines 123-139): Search icon on the left, X clear button on the right, same classes — `w-full pl-10 pr-10 py-2.5 text-sm bg-white/[0.06] border border-border/30 rounded-lg text-foreground placeholder:text-muted-foreground/40 focus:outline-none focus:border-border/60 transition-colors`. Add `mb-4` for spacing. (3) Import Search and X from lucide-react (add to existing import). (4) Filter: when searchQuery is non-empty, filter the `tracks` array. Match case-insensitively against: `t.title`, `t.sources?.some(s => s.title?.includes(q) || s.guest?.includes(q))`, `t.tags?.some(tag => tag.includes(q))`, `album?.title`, `t.quoteSpeaker`, `t.concept`. Apply filter AFTER sort. (5) Update the track count: when searching show '12 of 88 tracks', otherwise just '88 tracks'. Use a `filteredTracks` variable for the filtered result and render that instead of `tracks`. DONE: /songs has a working search bar. Searching 'Dr Becky' finds her tracks. Clearing shows all. DO NOT TOUCH: sort logic itself, track row rendering, play count logic, other pages. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Add search input UI between sort pills and column headers
Add filter logic with all specified fields
Create filteredTracks variable, render it instead of tracks
Update track count display for filtered state

features.description: Improve homepage search: expand filter fields AND change placeholder text. In app/page.tsx: (1) Change the search input placeholder (around line 128) from 'Search songs...' to 'What's your favorite Lenny podcast or article?'. (2) In the filteredTracks useMemo (around lines 31-43), expand the filter predicate. Currently matches: t.title, t.genre, t.tags, album.title. ADD these additional || conditions: `(t.sources && t.sources.some(s => (s.title && s.title.toLowerCase().includes(q)) || (s.guest && s.guest.toLowerCase().includes(q))))` for source titles and guest names, `(t.quoteSpeaker && t.quoteSpeaker.toLowerCase().includes(q))` for quote attribution, `(t.concept && t.concept.toLowerCase().includes(q))` for track concept, `(t.keyInsight && t.keyInsight.toLowerCase().includes(q))` for key insight. DONE: Placeholder reads the new text. Searching 'Dr Becky' finds her tracks. Searching a podcast episode title finds the song. DO NOT TOUCH: search UI layout (only placeholder text), debounce logic, popular tracks, album grid. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Change search placeholder text on the input element
Add sources[].title and sources[].guest to filter with .some()

features.description:
Create a first-time visitor onboarding component and mount it on the homepage. TWO STEPS: (A) Create components/OnboardingTooltips.tsx — a 'use client' component. On mount, check localStorage for `lgh-onboarding-seen` — if present, render nothing (return null). Otherwise render an inline card (NOT a modal). Design: div with classes `relative bg-white/[0.04] border border-border/30 rounded-lg px-5 py-4 mb-6`. Content: (1) A dismiss X button absolutely positioned top-right — on click sets `localStorage.setItem('lgh-onboarding-seen', '1')` and hides the component (use useState for visibility). (2) A heading: `

Welcome to Lenny's Greatest Hits 🎵

`. (3) Four tips in a 2x2 grid (grid grid-cols-2 gap-2): each tip is a `
` with an emoji span and text span. Tips: '🔺 Tap the play triangle to preview a song', '📄 Tap the song row to see lyrics & learnings', '👍 Vote thumbs up/down to give Eric feedback', '🔗 Share your favorites — it helps a lot!'. (4) Footer text: `

Have fun exploring! 🎵

`. (5) Wrap the entire card in framer-motion: ``. Import motion from 'framer-motion'. (B) In app/page.tsx, import OnboardingTooltips and mount it between the closing tag and the search bar div (before the 'relative mb-8' div). DONE: First-time visitors see a friendly welcome card with 4 tips. Dismissing sets localStorage. Return visitors don't see it. Card animates in smoothly. DO NOT TOUCH: lib/audio-context.tsx, other pages, NowPlayingBar. No new dependencies. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Add visibility state and dismiss handler
Add the 4 tips in 2x2 grid with emoji icons
Import and mount in app/page.tsx between header and search bar

features.description:
Improve the search 'no results' state on the homepage. In app/page.tsx, when search returns 0 results (around line 169-171), it currently shows just 'No songs found.' — a dead end. FIX: Replace the `

No songs found.

` element with a more helpful multi-line message. Use a div with class `mt-4 space-y-1`: (1) `

No songs found for "{debouncedQuery}".

` (2) `

We have {allTracks.length} songs covering topics like product-market fit, growth, leadership, and more.

` (3) `

Try searching for a guest name or topic!

`. DONE: Empty search state shows the user's query, total count, and a helpful suggestion. DO NOT TOUCH: search filter logic, track rendering, other pages. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Read app/page.tsx around the 'No songs found' text
Replace with the 3-line helpful message using debouncedQuery and allTracks.length

features.description: Research and write annotation drafts for batch 7 song: the-giant-piece-of-shit. (1) Read lyrics from content/songs/batch7/lyrics/the-giant-piece-of-shit.txt. (2) Read research brief from content/songs/batch7/research/the-giant-piece-of-shit.md. (3) Use mcp__lennysdata__search_content to find the source material this song is based on. (4) Use mcp__lennysdata__read_excerpt to pull specific quotes, context, and facts. (5) Identify 3-5 lyric lines that reference specific concepts, people, frameworks, or stories from the source. (6) Write annotation objects: { lyricText (exact substring from lyrics, case-insensitive partial match is fine), note (2-3 sentences explaining what this lyric references — specific, factual, connecting lyric to Lenny's content), sourceTitle (name of the article/episode), sourceUrl (URL if available, empty string if not) }. (7) Save to content/songs/batch7/annotations/the-giant-piece-of-shit.json as a JSON array. DONE: JSON file exists with 3-5 high-quality annotation objects. Each lyricText substring exists in the lyrics file. DO NOT TOUCH: lib/albums.ts, component files, other songs. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Create content/songs/batch7/annotations/ directory if it doesn't exist
Use mcp__lennysdata__search_content to find the source material
Use mcp__lennysdata__read_excerpt for quotes and context
Verify each lyricText appears in lyrics
